# The pre-commit hook loads with no-disk verify so it can self-heal an on-disk
# root-hash mismatch (a sealed object restored without its audit log). Its seal
# re-records the current state, so the commit is not blocked by a stale root
# hash. We create the mismatch with a plain copy so no hook fires early.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
cp secret.env app.env
exec sesam add app.env --group admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'v1 of the secret'
cp .sesam/objects/app.env.sesam v1.sealed

# Rotate + re-seal (new object + new seal root hash).
cp secret2.env app.env
exec sesam seal

# Restore the v1 object underneath the (v2) log -> stale on-disk root hash.
cp v1.sealed .sesam/objects/app.env.sesam

# A normal full verify fails on the mismatch…
! exec sesam verify
stderr 'root hash mismatch'

# …but pre-commit loads no-disk, re-seals the working-tree secret, and thereby
# reconciles the log instead of aborting the commit.
exec sesam hook pre-commit
exec sesam verify

-- secret.env --
TOKEN=super-secret
-- secret2.env --
TOKEN=rotated-secret
